description: dae eBPF transparent proxy — full metrics dashboard (Phase 1 gauges + Phase 2 counters/histograms)

panels.description: Number of alive proxy dialers (network=tcp4, all groups)
panels.title: Alive Dialers

panels.description: Current active TCP connections being proxied through dae
panels.title: Active TCP Conn

panels.description: DNS queries per second handled by dae
panels.title: DNS Query Rate

panels.description: Fraction of DNS queries served from cache (fresh hits only). Stale/lazy hits excluded.
panels.title: DNS Cache Fresh Hit Ratio

panels.description: Current active UDP endpoint associations and task queues
panels.title: Active UDP

panels.description: Process resident memory (RSS)
panels.title: Memory (RSS)

panels.description: Number of alive dialers per group for the selected network type. Drop to 0 = group is unusable.
panels.title: Alive Dialers by Group (network=$network)

panels.description: Most recent health check round-trip latency per dialer, displayed in milliseconds. Zero means no health check data yet (e.g., block/direct built-ins).
panels.title: Last Health Check Latency (network=$network)

panels.description: EWMA (thick line) smooths out noise from individual health checks. Instantaneous (thin) shows raw last-check latency. EWMA = current best estimate of dialer health.
panels.title: Latency: EWMA vs Instantaneous (network=$network)

panels.description: DNS query rate broken down by cache outcome: fresh hit (served from valid cache), lazy/stale hit (served from expired cache while refreshing), and miss (forwarded to upstream).
panels.title: DNS Query & Cache Breakdown

panels.description: Fresh hit ratio = cache_hit / total_queries. Total hit ratio includes stale (lazy) hits served during background refresh. Lower fresh hit ratio with higher total hit ratio indicates active background refresh.
panels.title: DNS Cache Hit Ratio

panels.description: DNS upstream forwarding attempt and error rates per upstream server. High error rates indicate upstream availability issues.
panels.title: DNS Upstream Query & Error Rate

panels.description:
DNS query concurrency: slots currently in use vs. total limit (default 16384). When in_use approaches limit, new queries are refused. 
  - DNS 查询极快（从常规数据看，47% 的查询在 1ms 内完成，71% 在 25ms 内完成）
  - Prometheus 抓取间隔通常 15s 或更长，几乎不可能"踩中"一个正在进行中的请求
  - 只有在上游极慢或并发爆炸时，这个值才会持续非零
panels.title: DNS Concurrency Slots

panels.description: DNS cache entries currently cached, forwarder connections cached, and refused/rejected query counts.
panels.title: DNS Cache State & Error Events

panels.description: End-to-end DNS handling latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99). Measured from query receipt to response sent, including cache lookup, forwarding, and response processing.
panels.title: DNS Response Latency (end-to-end)

panels.description: DNS upstream round-trip latency percentiles per upstream server. Measures time from forwarding request to receiving response.
panels.title: DNS Upstream Latency (per upstream)

panels.description: Number of in-flight DNS requests currently being forwarded to each upstream. Persistently high values suggest slow upstream or high concurrency.
panels.title: DNS Forwarder In-Flight (per upstream)

panels.description: TCP proxy connection establishment rate per outbound group and protocol. Shows proxy throughput — each increment is a new successfully established proxied connection.
panels.title: TCP Connection Establishment Rate

panels.description: Current active TCP connections, UDP endpoint associations, and UDP task queues. These are instantaneous gauges, not rates.
panels.title: Active Connection Pools

panels.description: UDP new endpoint association rate per group and protocol. Each increment is a new NAT mapping created for a UDP flow.
panels.title: UDP New Endpoint Rate

panels.description: Cumulative total TCP and UDP connections proxied since last restart (or SIGUSR1 reload). Resets on reload — use rate() for throughput.
panels.title: Cumulative Connection Totals (resets on reload)

panels.description: CPU time consumed per second. Value of 1.0 = one full CPU core. Values > number of cores indicate compute saturation.
panels.title: CPU Usage

panels.description: Go heap memory: allocated (in-use objects) vs. in-use heap spans. Sustained growth between GC cycles indicates memory pressure.
panels.title: Memory

panels.description: Number of goroutines. Sudden spikes may indicate goroutine leak. Gradual growth under load is normal.
panels.title: Goroutines

panels.description: GC pause duration percentiles. p99 > 10ms may cause latency spikes in proxy handling. Consider GOGC tuning if consistently high.
panels.title: GC Pause Duration

title: dae Transparent Proxy
